name: Spanish Almond Cake Laced with Tequila Anejo
recipeInstructions:
---------------------------------GARNISHES---------------------------------
Powdered sugar
Freshly grated nutmeg
Sliced mangoes (optional)
---------------------------MEXICAN WHIPPING CREAM---------------------------
1/2 pint heavy cream
3 tablespoons powdered sugar
1/4 teaspoon vanilla extract
1 tablespoon tequila anejo
Place the almonds on a baking sheet in a 325-degree preheated oven and toast until light brown, turning occasionally. Remove from the pan to prevent overcooking.
Finely grind all the almonds. Set aside 2 tablespoons of the ground nuts. Mix the remaining nuts with flour, baking powder, and salt, then set aside.
Cream the butter and sugar with an electric mixer. Add the eggs one at a time, mixing well after each addition. Blend in the almond extract, tequila, citrus zests, and 1/4 teaspoon grated nutmeg. Mix in the almond/flour mixture until incorporated. Spread the batter into a 9 ½-inch-by-2-inch pan (or a springform pan) that has been buttered and dusted with flour. Sprinkle with the remaining ground almonds.
Place in the preheated 325-degree oven on the middle rack and bake until a tester comes out clean, about 40-45 minutes. Cool for 10 minutes, then remove from the pan by inverting it onto a platter. Sprinkle with powdered sugar and freshly grated nutmeg. Serve with a coffee drink or eggnog.
Nutritional analysis per serving: 420 calories, 28 grams fat, 34 grams carbohydrates, 135 milligrams cholesterol, 189 milligrams sodium, 61 percent of calories from fat.
Mexican whipped cream:
In a chilled stainless-steel mixing bowl with chilled beaters, whip the cream until it thickens slightly. Slowly add powdered sugar, vanilla, and tequila, then beat until it forms stiff mounds.
Nutritional analysis per serving: 49 calories, 5 grams fat, 1 gram carbohydrates, 17 milligrams cholesterol, 5 milligrams sodium, 86 percent of calories from fat.
